task_completion.description: Open the FZK-Haus residential building model in the BIM authoring tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport displays a residential building model (FZK-Haus) with visible doors and architectural elements.

task_completion.description: Model geometry for at least three door hardware accessories using simple shapes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport or outliner shows at least three newly created simple geometric shapes (e.g., cylinders, boxes) intended to represent door handles, closers, or panic bars.

task_completion.description: Assign the IFC class 'IfcDiscreteAccessory' to the newly modeled hardware.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The object properties or IFC classification panel shows 'IfcDiscreteAccessory' assigned to the selected hardware objects.

task_completion.description: Position the hardware accessories so they sit on or immediately adjacent to existing doors (within 1.5 meters).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows the modeled hardware objects placed directly on or very close to the door panels in the building model.

task_completion.description: Nest each accessory into its respective 'IfcDoor' parent element.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The project outliner or IFC spatial tree shows the hardware objects (IfcDiscreteAccessory) nested as children under 'IfcDoor' parent elements.

task_completion.description: Define and associate a metallic material with the hardware accessories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The material properties panel shows a material assigned to the hardware with a name containing 'Steel', 'Metal', 'Aluminium', 'Brass', 'Iron', or 'Chrome'.

task_completion.description: Save the enriched project to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file explorer, or terminal command confirms the file is being saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_doors_detailed.ifc'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the BIM software's GUI or internal Python console to model and classify the objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Blender/Bonsai interface being actively used (menus open, objects selected, properties modified) rather than just a text editor modifying an IFC file directly.

integrity.description: The agent created actual 3D geometry for the accessories, not just empty IFC nodes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible 3D meshes (handles, bars, etc.) are present in the viewport, not just text entries in the outliner.

integrity.description: The agent created new objects rather than just reclassifying existing building elements as accessories.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original doors remain intact as IfcDoor, and new distinct objects are added as IfcDiscreteAccessory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must create at least 3 door hardware accessories. They must be assigned the IFC class 'IfcDiscreteAccessory' and nested under 'IfcDoor' elements. The material assigned must contain one of the following keywords: Steel, Metal, Aluminium, Aluminum, Brass, Iron, or Chrome. The final file must be saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_doors_detailed.ifc.
